content.full_text:
September 24, 1990

Jonathan Rothman, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Forest and Wildlife Protection Committee - Yes on Proposition 130

Dear Mr. Rothman:

The purpose of this letter is to confirm the telephone advice I received from you September 19, 1990.

I advised you that I serve as treasurer for the above referenced committee, which is organized for the purpose of promoting the passage of Proposition 130. The committee anticipates disseminating "advertisements" as that term is used in Government Code § 84503.

I further advised that the committee has received contributions from Mr. H. L. Arbit in excess of $500,000.00. In addition, Mr. Arbit is the largest contributor to the committee. Mr. Arbit's profession is an investment adviser. The committee has received no other contributions from persons in the same occupation.

In addition, the committee has not received contributions from corporations or unions as a group, or out of state contributors within the meaning of Government Code § 84507 (c) and (d), respectively.

To comply with the requirements of Government Code § 84507, et seq. the committee is disclosing on its advertisements as a "major funding source, H. L. Arbit." Mr. Arbit is being disclosed as a "person" under section 84507 (b). You advised me that this was a correct disclosure and complied with the provisions of Proposition 105.

Jonathan Rothman, Esq.
September 24, 1990
Page 2

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

LANCE H. OLSON

LHO/kl

cc: Barry Fadem, Esq.
